attacks.name: Surprise Thunder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, flip another coin. If the second coin is heads, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If the second coin is tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Stores up electricity in its body, then suddenly releases it to surprise and shock everyone.

rules:
If you play this card from your hand, remove 1 damage counter from the Pokémon you attach it to, if it has any.

Potion Energy provides Colorless energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

If you play this card from your hand, the Pokémon you attach it to is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.

Full Heal Energy provides Colorless energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

Attach Rainbow Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Rainbow Energy counts as every type of basic Energy but only provides 1 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card when not in play.) When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, it does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.)
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
All Pokémon Powers stop working until the end of your opponent's next turn.
Choose up to 3 Basic Pokémon cards, Evolution cards, and/or basic Energy cards from your discard pile. Show them to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.
Discard a card from your hand in order to play this card. Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck, then draws 4 cards.

Flip a coin. If tails, do 10 damage to your Active Pokémon. If heads, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, your opponent does 10 damage to his or her Active Pokémon. If heads, you flip a coin. Keep doing this until a player gets tails.

Ask your opponent if he or she accepts your challenge. If your opponent declines (or if both Benches are full), draw 2 cards. If your opponent accepts, each of you searches your decks for any number of Basic Pokémon cards and puts them face down onto your Benches. (A player can't do this if his or her Bench is full.) When you both have finished, shuffle your decks and turn those cards face up.
Search your deck for an Evolution card with Dark in its name. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Look at your opponent's hand. If he or she has any Trainer cards, choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.

fullDisplayName: Here Comes Team Rocket! 71/82 Team Rocket
rules: Each player plays with his or her Prize cards face up for the rest of the game.

flavorText: Lives in dark caves. Can occasionally be seen at night, when it flies in search of food.
Usually seen only in power plants, it is sometimes seen in towns, apparently using the power lines to travel.
Cute Pokémon that charms people with its innocent eyes. Loves swimming all day long.

attacks.name: Afternoon Nap
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Psychic Energy card and attach it to Slowpoke. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Although it knows how to fish, no other intelligent behavior has been detected in it.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

flavorText: Loves to eat walnuts, chestnuts, cheese, and milk.

attacks.name: Dizziness
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Psyduck but not used to pay for this attack. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

flavorText: It is often seen holding its head in its paws, as if it were suffering from a headache.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Ponyta in order to use this attack.

flavorText: A gentle grass-eater, it will allow riders on its back. However, anyone attempting to do so must be careful not to get burned.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It wanders around at night sowing its seeds to create more Oddishes.

attacks.name: Coin Hurl
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: A popular Pokémon that earns money for its owner with its "Pay Day" ability.

attacks.name: Mischief
attacks.effect: Shuffle your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Anger
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

flavorText: Normally friendly, it quickly becomes angry if it doesn't get what it wants.

attacks.name: Magnetism
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Magnemite, Magneton, and Dark Magneton on your Bench.

flavorText: It is said that enough Magnemites and Magnetons gather in one place, they can create a powerful magnetic barrier.
It's said that not even pro wrestlers can take down a Machop.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: First discovered in a weapons factory, this Pokémon can now be found almost anywhere.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Sticky Hands
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

flavorText: It has recently been found that Grimers are formed in waste-processing plants, and can move into towns via sewers.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: A carnivore that swallows its prey whole. Pidgeys and Spearows are its favorite food.

attacks.name: Sand-attack
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: A rare and unique Pokémon about which little is known, as few have been captured.

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Not popular with girls because of its attitude. Can often be heard muttering to itself in a low voice.

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The capture of the first Dratini inspired the search for other legendary Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dig Under
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: It follows Onixes in their tunnels, looking for scraps of food they have left behind.

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: A Raticate's front teeth are incredibly powerful; it can destroy a two-story house with them in 5 minutes.
Many people find it cute and try to hold it, only to be burned by its tail.

attacks.name: Vanish
attacks.effect: Shuffle Abra into your deck. (Discard all cards attached to Abra.)

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It teleports itself away from danger, making it difficult to capture.

attacks.name: Conversion 1
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has a Weakness, you may change it to a type of your choice other than Colorless.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: More and more people play the slots every day, trying to win a Porygon.

attacks.name: Rapid Evolution
attacks.effect: Search your deck for an Evolution card named Gyarados or Dark Gyarados and put it on Magikarp. (This counts as evolving Magikarp.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Recent research has revealed the presence of dragon DNA in this species of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Mirror Shell
attacks.effect: If an attack does damage to Dark Wartortle during your opponent's next turn (even if Dark Wartortle is Knocked Out), Dark Wartortle attacks the Defending Pokémon for an equal amount of damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon with a negative attitude, it hides in its shell whenever it's in a bad mood—which is often.

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

flavorText: A Pokémon capable of controlling water, so it is capable of creating giant whirlpools.

attacks.name: Flame Pillar
attacks.effect: You may discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Dark Rapidash when you use this attack. If you do and if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A wild, uncontrollable Pokémon. When running, it looks like a comet streaking through the sky.

attacks.name: Frenzied Attack
attacks.effect: Dark Primeape is now Confused (after doing damage).

flavorText: Although Primeapes have always been mean, lately they seem to be becoming even more vicious and frenzied.

attacks.name: Fascinate
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon. This attack can't be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Popular with women because of its beautiful fur. The leader of the Rockets keeps one as a pet.

attacks.name: Sludge Punch
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Muks appear suddenly, spreading sludge everywhere. Whenever one appears in a city, panic ensues.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon. Do the damage to the new Defending Pokémon. This attack can't be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Knock Back
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

flavorText: While normally peaceful and friendly, the number of incidents involving cruel Machokes is rising.

attacks.name: Mind Shock
attacks.effect: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: Some people say that Kadabras are responsible for many of the mysterious events reported in the news.

attacks.name: Lightning Flash
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Thunder Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, Dark Jolteon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: A dangerous Pokémon when angered. It likes to playfully shock people with the electricity it stores within its body.

attacks.name: Third Eye
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Dark Golduck in order to draw up to 3 cards.

flavorText: When Psyducks evolve, their headaches disappear and they find themselves in possession of formidable mental powers.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its pollen has such a strong smell that it even confuses itself at times.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Dark Flareon.

attacks.name: Playing with Fire
attacks.effect: Use this attack only if there are any Fire Energy cards attached to Dark Flareon. Flip a coin. If heads, discard 1 of those Energy cards and this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon with a hot temper that can't be controlled once it's angered. Capable of breathing flames hotter than 3000°.

attacks.name: Energy Bomb
attacks.effect: Take all Energy cards attached to Dark Electrode and attach them to your Benched Pokémon (in any way you choose). If you have no Benched Pokémon, discard all Energy cards attached to Dark Electrode.

flavorText: Some researchers hypothesize that Electrode may actually be a form of energy instead of having an actual body.

attacks.name: Tail Strike
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

flavorText: As is fitting for a legendary Pokémon, it has many strange powers, including the ability to make rain or snow fall.

attacks.name: Fireball
attacks.effect: Use this attack only if there are any Fire Energy cards attached to Dark Charmeleon. Flip a coin. If heads, discard 1 of those Energy cards. If tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

flavorText: Its tail is incredibly strong, able to lift up to 5 adult men.

attacks.name: Mass Explosion
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage times the total number of Koffings, Weezings, and Dark Weezings in play (Apply Weakness and Resistance.). Then, this attack does 20 damage to each Koffing, Weezing, and Dark Weezing (even your own). Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

attacks.name: Stun Gas
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned; if tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Researchers have observed these Pokémon reproducing in industrial waste sites.

attacks.name: Petal Whirlwind
attacks.effect: Flip a coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. If you get 2 or more heads, Dark Vileplume is now Confused (after doing damage).

flavorText: Perhaps because of their strong-smelling pollen, other Pokémon do not approach areas where Vileplumes live.

attacks.name: Fickle Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It loves nothing better than to be left alone, and so sometimes ends up being manipulated by unscrupulous people.

attacks.name: Sonicboom
attacks.effect: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

attacks.name: Magnetic Lines
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has any basic Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them. If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and attach that Energy card to it.

flavorText: The number of incidents of hackers using Magnetons to damage computer systems is steadily increasing.

attacks.name: Fling
attacks.effect: Your opponent shuffles his or her Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck. This attack can't be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It is almost impossible to defend against a Machamp's four powerful arms.

attacks.name: Bench Manipulation
attacks.effect: Your opponent flips a number of coins equal to the number of Pokémon on his or her Bench. This attack does 20 damage times the number of tails. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: Carries a pendulum with it always. Sometimes appears near less-traveled roads and schools at dusk.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Normally found only in the deep ocean, it has recently been seen in shallow waters as well.

attacks.name: Flitter
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: Sneaks up silently on its prey and attacks it from behind, sometimes killing it with one bite.

attacks.name: Knock Down
attacks.effect: Your opponent flips a coin. If tails, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if heads, this attack does 20 damage.

flavorText: Digs large sinkholes in the ground and tries to trap its enemies in them.

attacks.name: Giant Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Sometimes called "The God of Destruction." Its wings are able to support it in spite of its massive weight.

attacks.name: Continuous Fireball
attacks.effect: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Fire Energy cards attached to Dark Charizard. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads. Discard a number of Fire Energy cards attached to Dark Charizard equal to the number of heads.

flavorText: Seemingly possessed, it spews fire like a volcano, trying to burn all it sees.

attacks.name: Hydrocannon
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Dark Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack. You can't add more than 40 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Rocket Tackle
attacks.effect: Dark Blastoise does 10 damage to itself. Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Dark Blastoise during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: An aggressive Pokémon that tackles anything it sees.

attacks.name: Stare
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.) If that Pokémon has a Pokémon Power, that power stops working until the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Poison Vapor
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Freezes its prey with its stare. If you should encounter one, remember not to look into its eyes.

attacks.name: Teleport Blast
attacks.effect: You may switch Dark Alakazam with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Mind Shock
attacks.effect: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: Almost as if it were being controlled by something else, it never changes expressions, even in the middle of battle.

rules: Attach Rainbow Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Rainbow Energy counts as every type of basic Energy but only provides 1 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card when not in play.) When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, it does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.)
Look at your opponent's hand. If he or she has any Trainer cards, choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.
